data.text: a place or region beyond an enclosure or boundary: such as
examples: He looked forward to life on the outside again.
The outside of the house needs painting.
The candy was hard on the outside but chewy on the inside.
I may look calm on the outside, but I'm actually nervous.
the crowd numbered 10,000 at the outside

data.text: the world beyond the confines of an institution (such as a prison)
examples: He looked forward to life on the outside again.
The outside of the house needs painting.
The candy was hard on the outside but chewy on the inside.
I may look calm on the outside, but I'm actually nervous.
the crowd numbered 10,000 at the outside

data.text: the world beyond the territory or state of Alaska; especially: the 48 contiguous states
examples: He looked forward to life on the outside again.
The outside of the house needs painting.
The candy was hard on the outside but chewy on the inside.
I may look calm on the outside, but I'm actually nervous.
the crowd numbered 10,000 at the outside

data.text: of, relating to, or being on or toward the outer side or surface
examples: the outside edge
an outside pitch
turned on the outside light
outside telephone line
not taking any outside calls
blamed the riot on outside agitators
outside interests
an outside chance
borrowed a basketball and practiced his outside shot

data.text: of, relating to, or being on or toward the outer side of a curve or turn
examples: the outside edge
an outside pitch
turned on the outside light
outside telephone line
not taking any outside calls
blamed the riot on outside agitators
outside interests
an outside chance
borrowed a basketball and practiced his outside shot

data.text: of, relating to, or being on or near the outside
examples: the outside edge
an outside pitch
turned on the outside light
outside telephone line
not taking any outside calls
blamed the riot on outside agitators
outside interests
an outside chance
borrowed a basketball and practiced his outside shot
The car seemed in good condition outside.
children playing outside
The car seemed in good condition outside.
children playing outside

data.text: —used as a function word to indicate movement to or position on the outer side of
examples: outside the scope of this report
outside the law
No one knew outside a few friends.
outside the scope of this report
outside the law
No one knew outside a few friends.
outside the scope of this report
outside the law
No one knew outside a few friends.
lived outside of the city
Outside of the players, no one was allowed in the locker room.
lived outside of the city
Outside of the players, no one was allowed in the locker room.

data.text: a person whose opinion, perspective, etc. differs from one's own or from that of a particular group; also: an opinion, perspective, etc. that differs from one's own or that of a particular group
examples: By being an objective outside voice, the board chair can reduce the likelihood of extreme performance outcomes …

Convinced I was right, I sought an outside voice: Judge John Hodgman, the comedian who writes a satiric ethical advice column for The New York Times Magazine. I explained our impasse in an email, and Hodgman replied in the May 20 issue …
The hard part about relationships is just trying to be in one without a million outside voices interfering.
My personal insight and advice to you is … to quiet outside voices, grow a backbone, and to be in charge of your life …
This is a painstakingly crafted treatise on what it's like to live inside your own mind in an age where millions of outside voices are bombarding that mind endlessly.
… a long piece, with some good outside voices in it, was unplanned and written in one intense sprint.
… corrections like “sit still” or “you're using your outside voice, please use your inside voice” …
… at exactly the moment we were walking past a lineup at the check-out, in all innocence, she [a young child] used her loud outside voice and asked: "What colour are your panties, Grandma?"

data.text: a volume of speaking that is typically considered too loud to be used indoors (as at home or school)
examples: By being an objective outside voice, the board chair can reduce the likelihood of extreme performance outcomes …

Convinced I was right, I sought an outside voice: Judge John Hodgman, the comedian who writes a satiric ethical advice column for The New York Times Magazine. I explained our impasse in an email, and Hodgman replied in the May 20 issue …
The hard part about relationships is just trying to be in one without a million outside voices interfering.
My personal insight and advice to you is … to quiet outside voices, grow a backbone, and to be in charge of your life …
This is a painstakingly crafted treatise on what it's like to live inside your own mind in an age where millions of outside voices are bombarding that mind endlessly.
… a long piece, with some good outside voices in it, was unplanned and written in one intense sprint.
… corrections like “sit still” or “you're using your outside voice, please use your inside voice” …
… at exactly the moment we were walking past a lineup at the check-out, in all innocence, she [a young child] used her loud outside voice and asked: "What colour are your panties, Grandma?"
